title: Rhabdophyllum buchholzii
taxonomy: Rhabdophyllum buchholzii is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central Africa. It is a member of the genus Rhabdophyllum, which is part of the larger family Sapindaceae. The plant is also known as the African bush mango. Rhabdophyllum buchholzii belongs to the class Magnoliopsida, the subclass Rosidae, and the order Sapindales. It is further classified into the family Sapindaceae and the genus Rhabdophyllum.

intro: Rhabdophyllum buchholzii, a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae, is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rhabdophyllum buchholzii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Rhabdophyllum buchholzii is often used to make various herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments.
